pattern: Counting node values across the whole tree and keeping every value whose count ties the running maximum reduces mode-finding to a single tally pass.
hints: Every duplicate value contributes to a frequency tally; the answer is whichever value or values occur the most times.
Walk the tree once, counting how often each value appears.
Track the highest frequency seen, then collect all values that reach that frequency.

edge_inputs.description: single node is its own mode
duplicate placed in the right subtree
two distinct values each appearing once both qualify as modes
all nodes share the same value
left-skewed BST with a clear single mode
tie between two values with equal repeats

stress.description: random BST built from a multiset so duplicates and ties arise naturally
stress.note: BST insertion of a multiset keeps the BST-with-duplicates property

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def findMode(self, root):
        from collections import Counter
        cnt = Counter()
        stack = [root]
        while stack:
            node = stack.pop()
            if node is None:
                continue
            cnt[node.val] += 1
            stack.append(node.left)
            stack.append(node.right)
        best = max(cnt.values())
        return [v for v, c in cnt.items() if c == best]
